location: Rare drop (2% chance) from pickaxe-wielding Miner enemies found in mining tunnels across the map; most efficiently farmed in the Limgrave Tunnels.
tactics: Enter the Limgrave Tunnels and clear the group of five Miners near the second elevator shaft, past the first elevator, for a tight farming loop.
After killing them, jump down the elevator shaft to die and respawn at the Limgrave Tunnels grace, then repeat.
Don't confuse regular Miners with Glintstone Miners — the latter carry a Digger's Staff instead and won't drop the Pickaxe.
Unusually for a Great Hammer, the Pickaxe deals Pierce damage and uses the Greataxe moveset, so it won't stance-break the way most hammers do, and it can't be power-stanced with other Great Hammers.
lore: A large pickaxe made for cracking bedrock, favored by sturdy miners. It was never meant as a weapon, but its point proves surprisingly effective at punching through armor.

builds.name: Anti-armor pierce build
builds.why: D-scaling Str/Dex plus its unusual Pierce damage type (rare among Great Hammers) lets it exploit enemies resistant to Strike damage, at the cost of the usual hammer stance-break bonus.
